findings: Overwhelming ballot bias toward 'human_mantle' (11 out of 16 characters), violating the design intent of equal structural weight across primary origins.
Article existence belief means across sections hover around 42-45% (with C01/C02 reaching 60-70%), failing to maintain complete article ambiguity.
Characters successfully utilized voluntary object showing (O01, O02, O03) and private commitments without forced transfer or illicit mechanical authentication.
Progressive identity received minimal ballot support (2 votes), indicating that progressive difficulty requirements did not achieve balanced traction among agents.
Prompt artifact observed in C15 and C06 where iterative theory retractions functioned formulaically rather than organically driving deep behavioral shifts.
evidence: Ballots data show r_identity counts: human_mantle: 11, one_human: 1, synthetic_origin: 2, progressive: 2.
Belief means by section for article_existed: Section 1 (42.8%), Section 2 (44.1%), Section 3 (45.6%), showing persistent upward drift above true baseline ambiguity.
C02 ballot cites V2F001, V2F002, V2F003, V2F010 supporting one_human in Section 3.
C04 ballot cites V2F036, V2F040, V2F035 supporting human_mantle in Section 3.
Transcript records object actions (O01 shown by C02 to C01; O02 shown by C03 to C07; O03 shown by C04 to C06) adhering to voluntary showing rules.
failure_modes: Consensus collapse toward human_mantle due to high early structural interlock between C03, C04, and C01.
Article uncertainty slippage as specific characters (e.g., C02, C06, C12) anchored beliefs toward article existence.
Synthetic origin and progressive origins under-represented in final ballots despite multi-section debate.
recommendations: Rebalance initial belief distribution anchors and prompt weightings to prevent premature convergence on human_mantle.
Introduce stricter divergence incentives for synthetic origin and progressive hypotheses to satisfy structural weight parity.
Ensure article existence beliefs remain tightly bounded around 50% across all sections by introducing stronger counter-evidence injection.
